summary: Small tooling fix in tools/wiki_tu.py: ColumnIndex.FILE was changed from auto() to an explicit 0, leaving later IntEnum members to continue with auto(). No PR body, comments, reviews, or validation output were present in the slice.

key_files.role: Python tooling script defining ColumnIndex; PR changes FILE enum value from auto() to 0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: class ColumnIndex(IntEnum): FILE changed from auto() to 0

what_changed.text: In tools/wiki_tu.py, ColumnIndex.FILE is now explicitly assigned 0 instead of auto(). MATCHED, TOTAL, and REMAINING still use auto().

smart_moves.text: The fix is narrowly scoped to the initial enum value, avoiding unrelated changes to wiki_tu.py or other tooling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: one added line and one deleted line

decomp_lessons.text: For Python tooling enums that represent column positions, verify whether the first value must be zero-based before relying on IntEnum auto().
evidence_refs: PR title: Fix column enum assignment in `wiki_tu.py`
raw_diff: ColumnIndex.FILE changed from auto() to 0

follow_up_queries: Python IntEnum auto zero based column index tooling

accepted_candidate_records.title: Set the first IntEnum value explicitly when a tooling enum models zero-based columns
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2652 fixed tools/wiki_tu.py by changing ColumnIndex.FILE from auto() to 0. When a Python IntEnum represents column indices, check whether the first member must be zero-based before using auto() for every member.
evidence_refs: PR title: Fix column enum assignment in `wiki_tu.py`
raw_diff: ColumnIndex.FILE changed from auto() to 0
